description: ITP workflow enforcement - implementation standards, documentation coupling, fake data guard, code correctness, SR&ED commit validation, process storm prevention, Vale terminology enforcement, GLOSSARY.md bidirectional sync, file size bloat prevention

hooks.description: Bash subprocess stdin inlet guard — pre-disconnects stdin via < /dev/null wrap on Bash invocations (iter-63 narrowed matcher from `*` to `Bash`: only Bash spawns the subprocess class with TTY-contention risk; non-Bash tools (Read/Glob/Grep/Edit/Write/mcp__*) save ~12-17ms cold-start each)

Iter-84/85/86/87/88/89/90 in-process orchestrator: combines multiple Write|Edit subhooks into ONE bun process, amortizing the EMPIRICAL ~17ms per-saved-subhook cold-start cost (iter-87 microbenchmark verified; supersedes iter-80's ~44ms estimate which included payload-handling overhead, matching the community 8-15ms benchmarks surfaced by iter-86 web research and Bun 1.3 single-digit-ms cold-start range surfaced by iter-89 web research). Per iter-87 empirical re-projection, final-state savings ≈ (8-1) × 17 = ~119ms per Write|Edit (not iter-81's 308ms projection). Inlined subhooks (registry-order, lightest-first deny-wins): version-guard, hoisted-deps-guard, mise-hygiene-guard, pyi-stub-guard, native-binary-guard, gpu-optimization-guard, file-size-guard. Iter-91 migrates the final Write|Edit subhook (vale-claude-md-guard) to complete the 8/8 PreToolUse migration arc. Belt-and-suspenders deny+ask defense per GH #37210 / iter-78 (stdout JSON + stderr + exitCode=2 with stdout-drain-await per iter-85 audit hardening). Process-level unhandledRejection fail-open handler. Per-subhook cooperative timeout via AbortSignal.timeout() (iter-87 refactor from Symbol-sentinel + raw setTimeout — idiomatic 2026 Web Platform API pattern). PreToolUse additionalContext silent-drop NON-USE invariant verified marketplace-wide (iter-90 audit per GitHub #15664). Subhook contract violations gated preventively by iter-86 audit task. Empirical savings curve continuously validated by iter-87 benchmark task.

Iter-78 edit-time companion to iter-77 release-time Check 4k — blocks edits that introduce references to ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/<segment>/ where <segment> is NOT in the cache-populator allowlist {hooks, skills, commands, agents, plugin.json}; belt-and-suspenders defense (stdout JSON deny + stderr diagnostic + exit 2) per GitHub #37210
Warns Claude when reading files >2000 lines without offset/limit
Nudge toward DuckDB for Parquet content analysis (soft, warn+allow)
Pueue auto-wrap + OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN injection — MUST be LAST PreToolUse entry (mitigates GitHub #15897 multi-hook updatedInput aggregation bug)

Bash subprocess orphan cleanup — removes zombie/TTY-holding orphans spawned by Bash invocations (iter-64 narrowed matcher from `*` to `Bash`: only Bash spawns subprocesses that can orphan; Read/Glob/Grep/Edit/Write are in-process; async:true from iter-57 already prevented user-latency cost, but matcher narrowing also eliminates ~12-17ms CPU+battery waste per non-Bash call)
SSoT/DI principles reminder with ast-grep detection (once per session)
Memory efficiency reminder - zero-copy, pre-allocate, cache locality, lazy eval (once per session)
ty type checker - runs on every .py file edit (4.7ms incremental)
tsgo type checker - runs on every .ts/.tsx file edit (~170ms project check)
oxlint correctness checker - runs on every .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx file edit (~50ms)
biome lint - complementary JS/TS checks (useConst, noDoubleEquals, node: protocol)

hooks.rewakeSummary: Loop stall detected — rewaking to schedule waker
hooks.description: Stop-hook orchestrator: sequentially runs the 5 itp-hooks Stop hooks (subprocess-cleanup, error-summary, ty-check, markdown-lint, loop-stall-guard) with per-subhook timeout + crash isolation. Replaces 5 entries in the Stop hook display list with 1. asyncRewake on exit 2 propagates from loop-stall-guard.
